metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the demote-on-alarm protocol co-located with R7's specification: what 'trailing FP' means (window size, measurement method), what the alarm mechanism is, and what 'demote' does operationally (which field in criteria_routing.json changes, from what value to what value, requiring what approval).
evidence: Plan text: 'R7 dogfood monitoring (alarm at trailing FP >10%, demote-on-alarm precedent T3/T10)'
criteria_routing.json T3 entry: has exec/checklist/routing fields but no demote-on-alarm protocol
criteria_routing.json T10 entry: similarly has no demote mechanism defined
Search for 'demote-on-alarm' in repo returns only plan-review cache files referencing this plan — not any implementation

ABSENCE: the alarm trigger condition (how trailing FP is measured, over what window), the alarm mechanism (what system fires it), and the demotion action (what field changes, who approves) are all unspecified
findings.finding: The Success Criteria reference a 'demote-on-alarm' protocol ('alarm at trailing FP >10%, demote-on-alarm precedent T3/T10') but neither T3 nor T10 have a demote-on-alarm protocol defined in their `criteria_routing.json` entries or reviewer prompts, and the operational meaning of 'demote' (flip `default_posture`? lower `block_threshold`? disable criterion?) is never specified anywhere in the plan or linked files.
findings.location: Success Criteria second bullet: 'alarm at trailing FP >10%, demote-on-alarm precedent T3/T10'
scenarios: R7 ships instrumentation but the operator doesn't know when to trigger a demotion or what to change — the alarm-to-demotion protocol exists only in this plan's prose.
A future reviewer of a T3/T10 finding cannot find the 'demote-on-alarm' precedent to understand what 'T3/T10 demoted' means in the sidecar.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to R6 (5dac) a docs/experiments/ writeup or a formally cited epic-gate-table artifact that records R6's exemption from the eval gate and its rationale, satisfying parent SC1/AC3 for this recommendation.
evidence: Parent SC1: 'Every implemented recommendation has a committed docs/experiments/ writeup showing its gate row passed before implementation'
Parent AC3: 'no recommendation implemented without its gate'
R6 What: 'carries no detection risk, so it ships first with no eval gate (per epic gate table)'
R6 has no ACs requiring a gate-row writeup or a docs/experiments/ writeup demonstrating passage
findings.finding: R6 (child 5dac-c177-56ff-4af4) ships without an eval gate row, yet parent SC1 requires 'every implemented recommendation has a committed docs/experiments/ writeup showing its gate row passed before implementation' and parent AC3 requires each recommendation 'landed through Gerrit with its gate row demonstrably passed … no recommendation implemented without its gate.' R6 has no gate row and no gate-row writeup, referencing only an 'epic gate table' that is not defined within the plan text or any child — the exemption is unverifiable from the plan as written.
scenarios: R6 lands directly; the completion verifier later flags the absence of a gate-row writeup; the parent epic cannot be closed with AC3 satisfied for R6.
An auditor challenges the 'epic gate table' exemption — the table is not committed or referenced by any child, making the exemption unverifiable.
findings.suggested_fix: Commit a one-page docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/r6-gate-rationale.md that cites the gate-table row, records the 'no detection risk / coaching-only' classification, and is referenced in R6's ACs so AC3 can be closed by inspection.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the 'gate row' schema (required fields: experiment id, metric name, observed value, pass/fail threshold, corpus reference, date) so AC3's 'demonstrably passed' is objectively verifiable, and specify where the 'gate table' document lives (e.g. `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/gate-table.md`).
evidence: Plan AC3: 'landed through Gerrit with its gate row demonstrably passed'
Plan Success Criteria: 'new criteria enter at advisory POSTURE first where the gate table says so'
Search of docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/ finds README.md, harnesses/, criteria/, runs/ — no 'gate table' document with a defined schema
criteria_routing.json has default_posture fields but no 'gate table' structure governing R1-R7 posture decisions
ABSENCE: no gate-row schema (required fields, pass condition format) is defined in the plan or any linked artifact
findings.finding: The plan's AC3 and Success Criteria refer to a 'gate row' that must be 'demonstrably passed' before each R1-R7 implementation, and to a 'gate table' that dictates advisory posture for new criteria — but neither 'gate row' nor 'gate table' is defined as a schema anywhere in the plan text or linked files. An executor implementing R1-R7 cannot know what fields a gate row must contain (pass/fail condition, metric value, corpus reference, date?) to count as 'demonstrably passed.'
findings.location: Success Criteria (second bullet) and AC3: 'gate row demonstrably passed'; Success Criteria: 'where the gate table says so'
scenarios: An executor writing up E2 results does not know whether 'gate row passed' requires a specific JSON format, a prose writeup, a threshold value, or a combination — leading to inconsistent evidence records across R1-R7 children.
A reviewer of an R-child ticket cannot objectively verify 'gate row demonstrably passed' without knowing what a gate row looks like.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/gate-table.md` with the gate-row schema (fields: `experiment_id`, `metric`, `observed`, `threshold`, `pass`, `corpus_ref`, `date`) and reference it from AC3. Each R-child's writeup must include a populated gate-row block.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either tag the ticket-comment requirement in AC2 with `[operator-attested]` (per ADR 0043) so it is met by a recorded attestation, or replace 'ticket comments' with an in-repo artifact (e.g., a RESULTS.md or a section in the docs/experiments/ writeup) that is verifiable via repo state.
evidence: 'closed with its results recorded as ticket comments AND a docs/experiments/ writeup' — the ticket-comment half is not in-repo
AC is not prefixed with [operator-attested], so the ADR 0043 exception does not apply
The docs/experiments/ writeup half IS in-repo verifiable; the ticket-comment half is not
findings.finding: AC2 requires results to be 'recorded as ticket comments', which is an external-system artifact (issue/ticket tracker), not a repo or CI artifact. This criterion is not tagged [operator-attested] (ADR 0043), so it cannot be verified in-session via repo artifacts or a deterministic command — an agent closing this story cannot confirm the AC is met without accessing the external ticket system.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC line 2: '…results recorded as ticket comments AND a docs/experiments/ writeup'
scenarios: An agent verifying AC completion can check the docs/experiments/ writeup but cannot programmatically confirm ticket comments exist without ticket-system access
A CI gate checking AC satisfaction would pass even if ticket comments were never written, making the criterion partially unenforceable
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'results recorded as ticket comments AND a docs/experiments/ writeup' to 'results recorded in the docs/experiments/ writeup (a RESULTS section listing gate row pass/fail outcomes)' — this makes the AC fully in-repo verifiable and removes the external-system dependency.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm `docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md` (commit 143d5074e) is accessible at implementation time and that each R1–R7 section contains a concrete, self-contained gate row (metric + threshold + corpus) that can be evaluated without referencing ticket englacial-communal-conure/d39b; if the doc is not yet committed or the ticket is not yet created, add an AC requiring it be committed before any E-child closes.
evidence: Unverified: docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md not present in repo (FileNotFoundError on read)
Unverified: rebar show englacial-communal-conure/d39b → 'Ticket not found'
Plan text: '§6 defines R1-R7, each with a falsifiable experiment' — those definitions live only in the absent doc
Plan text: 'Implementation children… sequence after the floor-split story' — sequencing logic also derives from R-definitions in the absent doc
AC3: 'Each recommendation child (R1-R7) is either landed through Gerrit with its gate row demonstrably passed, or closed/deferred with the failing gate result recorded'
findings.finding: The plan's entire gate structure — including the falsifiable experiment definitions and pass/fail thresholds for every R1–R7 recommendation — derives from `docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md` (commit 143d5074e), which is absent from the current repo snapshot and whose linked ticket (englacial-communal-conure/d39b) returns 'not found'. AC3 requires each recommendation be 'landed through Gerrit with its gate row demonstrably passed', but the gate rows themselves are defined only in this unverifiable document. If the SOTA doc's experiment specifications are vague, contradictory, or unavailable at implementation time, the E-gate → implement → ship sequencing the plan depends on cannot be executed.
findings.location: Context section ('commit 143d5074e, ticket englacial-communal-conure/d39b') and AC item 3 ('gate row demonstrably passed')
scenarios: An implementer begins E1 but the SOTA doc's definition of its gate row (pass threshold, corpus size, kappa requirement) is ambiguous — they cannot determine when E1 is closed, blocking R1.
The SOTA doc defines a gate for R5 that requires a capability the current review_kernel does not expose; without reading the doc this gap is invisible until mid-implementation.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC0 (prerequisite): 'The SOTA report (docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md) is committed to the repo at a reachable ref AND each R1–R7 §6 entry contains: (a) a named metric, (b) a numeric pass threshold, (c) a corpus or data source. E1 may not close until this AC is met.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the absence of E4 in AC2: either add E4 to the experiment list with its scope, or add a parenthetical stating E4 does not exist in the SOTA report (e.g., '(E4 not defined in §6 — R4 has no separate eval child)')
evidence: 'Each experiment child (E1, E2, E3, E5, E6) is closed with its results recorded' — E4 absent from list with no rationale
The Context section references the SOTA report's §6 defining R1-R7 each with a 'falsifiable experiment' without clarifying whether E4 exists or was collapsed into another gate
findings.finding: AC2 enumerates experiment children as 'E1, E2, E3, E5, E6' with E4 absent and no explanation. An executor cannot determine whether E4 was intentionally omitted (does not exist in the SOTA report), merged into another experiment, or accidentally dropped — this ambiguity could cause an executor to either wait for E4 or incorrectly skip a required validation.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC line 2: 'Each experiment child (E1, E2, E3, E5, E6) is closed…'
scenarios: Executor implementing E-gate scaffolding creates an E4 placeholder for a non-existent experiment
Executor skips a real E4 experiment believing it was intentionally omitted
Plan reader auditing gate completeness cannot verify the set is exhaustive
findings.suggested_fix: In AC2, replace '(E1, E2, E3, E5, E6)' with '(E1, E2, E3, E5, E6 — E4 is not defined; R4 validation is subsumed by E3)' or whichever experiment actually covers R4, so the list is self-explanatory.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add E4 and E7 (or their correct named equivalents) to AC item 2's experiment-closure list, or explicitly document in the plan why those two recommendations share/omit a standalone experiment child.
evidence: AC item 2: 'Each experiment child (E1, E2, E3, E5, E6) is closed with its results recorded as ticket comments AND a docs/experiments/ writeup.'
Context: '§6 defines R1-R7, each with a falsifiable experiment.'
Context also calls out 'child E1 re-verifies' and 'R5 requires the E5 non-regression gate' — but E4 and E7 appear nowhere in the plan.
ABSENCE: E4 and E7 (or their named equivalents) are not mentioned anywhere in the plan text.
findings.finding: AC item 2 names only five experiment children (E1, E2, E3, E5, E6) but the Context states '§6 defines R1-R7, each with a falsifiable experiment,' implying at least seven experiments. E4 (and E7) are absent from the AC closure requirement, creating both an uncovered-work gap in the criteria (E1) and a cross-section contradiction between the Context's scope claim and the AC's enumeration (COH).
findings.location: AC item 2 vs. Context paragraph ('§6 defines R1-R7, each with a falsifiable experiment')
scenarios: An implementer closes E4's experiment (whichever recommendation it gates) and has no AC to satisfy, creating a gap in the done-definition for that recommendation's gate.
A reviewer checking AC completion ticks off E1,E2,E3,E5,E6 and considers the epic done, leaving R4's and R7's experimental closure unverified.
findings.suggested_fix: Amend AC item 2 to: 'Each experiment child (E1, E2, E3, E4, E5, E6, E7) is closed with its results recorded…' — or, if R4 and R7 use a different experiment naming convention, name them explicitly and add a note in Context clarifying the E-label → R-label mapping.

findings.finding: Sibling tickets touch the same file(s) with no ordering edge.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a depends_on/blocks edge to serialize, or partition the file ownership.

evidence: 3 criterion line(s) bundle multiple deliverables with 'and' (split so each is independently verifiable).
findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a contingency to R1 (59b7): 'If E1 re-verification revises the verified miss set, R1's gate-row fixture list is updated to match E1's frozen corpus before any detector run; the update is committed as a gate-row amendment with E1's revised numbers cited.'
evidence: R1 AC: 'dc58/db7b/5886 each surface the decisive finding above the advisory cap' as a gate-row requirement
Parent context: '§5 mined this store's own history ... child E1 re-verifies these claims before anything depends on them'
E1 AC: 'The §5 headline claims are re-verified against the frozen corpus and the ticket comment records agreement or corrected numbers'
R1 has no contingency AC for the case where E1 corrects the miss count or identity
findings.finding: R1 (59b7) hardcodes dc58, db7b, and 5886 as the three historical miss plans that 'each surface the decisive finding above the advisory cap' as a required gate-row leg. But E1 (e95e) is designed to re-verify the §5 headline claims and may correct the numbers — if E1 finds fewer than 3 confirmed misses in the defect class, or that one of the three is mis-classified, R1's gate row becomes structurally impossible (requiring all 3 to fire on plans that may not be verified misses). No child states what happens to R1's gate row if E1's re-verification revises the miss count or identity.
scenarios: E1 re-verification finds only 2 confirmed misses in the defect class (e.g., 5886 is reclassified as a different defect class); R1's gate row requires all 3 to fire but one plan no longer qualifies as a miss — the gate row is structurally unsatisfiable.

E1 finds that one of the three plans (e.g., dc58) does not exhibit the 'asserted capability' pattern at all; R1 cannot pass its own gate row and cannot be formally deferred with a 'failing gate result' since the gate criterion itself is invalid.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the hardcoded plan IDs in R1's AC with a reference to 'the verified miss plans in E1's frozen outcome corpus (defect class: asserted capability)' so the gate criterion dynamically tracks E1's output rather than pre-committing to specific IDs.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to R5 (ea39) an explicit coordination note with the calibration-3 epic (relishable-ammonitic-hoverfly) on IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump sequencing, or confirm via a cross-epic dependency that only one version bump train exists.
evidence: Parent Context: 'This epic runs alongside the calibration-3 line (task relishable-ammonitic-hoverfly, story large-sleepful-needlefish); implementation children that touch criteria_routing.json/review_kernel sequence after the floor-split story to avoid file conflicts'

R5 Scope: 'Sequenced AFTER the floor-split story (large-sleepful-needlefish) - both touch kernel decide + sidecar; the floor split may itself bump the version, in which case this composes into the same bump train'
No child mentions coordination with the calibration-3 epic for the version bump itself
findings.finding: R5 (ea39) bumps IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION (plan-v3) and notes the floor-split story may also bump it ('this composes into the same bump train'), but no child addresses coordination of this version bump with the concurrent calibration-3 epic (task relishable-ammonitic-hoverfly, story large-sleepful-needlefish) mentioned in the parent context as running alongside. If the calibration-3 line also bumps the version independently, two concurrent plan-v3 bumps could collide or produce a version that pools incompatible calibration corpora.
scenarios: calibration-3 epic and R5 both land plan-v3 bumps referring to different vocabulary states; historical corpora labeled plan-v3 now pool incompatible findings, invalidating calibration.
R5's IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump conflicts with a bump from the floor-split story that also changes the kernel; the 'same bump train' composition is undefined for three concurrent changes.
findings.suggested_fix: Establish a single version-bump authority (e.g., the floor-split story large-sleepful-needlefish) that both epics compose into, with R5 and calibration-3 both declared as dependents of that story's bump rather than issuing independent bumps.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit fallback protocol for inconclusive/unrunnable E-gate experiments: define what 'inconclusive' means (e.g., kappa unreachable after N rounds, baseline unavailable), who decides, and what happens to dependent R-children (defer? proceed with documented uncertainty? require a spike?).
evidence: AC1: 'inter-rater kappa >= 0.7 recorded' — but no fallback if kappa < 0.7 after N rounds
AC2: experiment must be 'closed with its results recorded' — but no protocol if the experiment cannot run (corpus unavailable, adjudicators disagree irreconcilably)
AC3: only two states defined: 'gate row demonstrably passed' OR 'failing gate result recorded' — the third state (inconclusive/unrunnable experiment) has no protocol

ABSENCE: no specification of what happens when an E-experiment sub-step fails (e.g., corpus freeze fails because kappa < 0.7, or the E5 non-regression gate cannot be established because there are no baselines)
findings.finding: No fallback is specified for the case where an E-gate experiment itself produces an unusable or ambiguous result — e.g., E1 fails to reach inter-rater kappa ≥ 0.7 after multiple adjudication rounds, or an E-gate metric is inconclusive (neither clear pass nor clear fail). The plan covers 'gate row passed → implement' and 'gate row failed → defer/record' but has no protocol for an experiment that cannot produce a valid gate row.
findings.location: AC2: 'Each experiment child (E1, E2, E3, E5, E6) is closed with its results recorded'; AC3: 'either landed... or closed/deferred with the failing gate result recorded'
scenarios: E1 corpus freeze: two annotators reach kappa 0.62 after three rounds. The plan gives no guidance on whether to lower the kappa threshold, bring in a third annotator, or abandon E1 entirely — blocking every downstream R that depends on the corpus.
E5 non-regression gate: no prior baseline exists for the review_kernel shared path. The gate 'cannot be established' — but the plan only covers pass/fail states, not this third state.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to E6 (a880) an explicit prerequisite: 'R3 must commit a candidate rubric on the experiment branch before E6's order-shuffle AC can execute'; add a reciprocal note in R3 (3a27).
evidence: E6 AC: 'Order-shuffle stability for the R3 container criterion is recorded (verdict agreement across >=3 section-order permutations on >=20 container plans)'
E6 Scope: 'candidate rubric on an experiment branch; no packaged changes land from this story'
R3 AC: 'Gate-row evidence committed before landing: ... E6 order-shuffle stability recorded for the rubric'
Neither child states 'R3 must draft candidate rubric before E6 runs' as an explicit dependency or ordering constraint
findings.finding: E6 (a880) must test order-shuffle stability for R3's new container criterion, but R3 (3a27) gates on E6 passing before landing. This creates an implicit coordination requirement: E6 needs a candidate rubric from R3 to run its order-shuffle test, but this dependency (R3 must draft its candidate rubric on an experiment branch before E6 can execute its second AC) is not stated in either child, leaving the ordering ambiguous and the experiment-branch handoff undocumented.
scenarios: E6 is assigned before R3 has drafted a candidate rubric; E6 cannot run its order-shuffle AC and blocks R3's gate row indefinitely.
E6 tests a stale or incorrect rubric draft, invalidating the gate-row evidence R3 depends on.
findings.suggested_fix: In E6's Scope section, add: 'Requires R3 candidate rubric committed on experiment branch (coordinate with R3 story owner before scheduling E6 run).' In R3's Scope, add: 'Candidate rubric must be committed on experiment branch to unblock E6.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit sequencing constraint to the parent epic (and to stenchful-retail-basenji) stating that R7 instrumentation must ship before or concurrently with the first Rn recommendation child, so the dogfood FP alarm is live before any new criteria enter production.
evidence: Success criteria: 'R7 dogfood monitoring (alarm at trailing FP >10%, demote-on-alarm precedent T3/T10)' — implies R7 must be present before other R-tickets can be safely monitored in production.

carmine-jute-starfish (R6) is labeled 'ship-first' in its child description, but stenchful-retail-basenji (R7) has no such label despite R7 being the monitoring infrastructure for all shipped recommendations.

The sequencing section in Context only addresses file-conflict ordering ('implementation children that touch criteria_routing.json/review_kernel sequence after the floor-split story') — it does not establish R7 as a prerequisite for other Rn children.
findings.finding: R7 (standing gate-eval instrumentation + dogfood FP monitoring) has no explicit 'ship-first' sequencing constraint in the parent epic, yet the success criteria make its alarm/demote-on-alarm safety net a prerequisite for the dogfood guarantee on all other R-tickets. If any Rn lands before R7, the trailing FP >10% alarm is absent during that window.
findings.location: Success Criteria paragraph 2 ('R7 dogfood monitoring (alarm at trailing FP >10%, demote-on-alarm precedent T3/T10)') and child stenchful-retail-basenji
scenarios: R1 or R2 ships before R7: a false-positive regression in production goes undetected because the trailing FP alarm does not yet exist.
R6 ships first (labeled 'ship-first') but R7 has not yet landed, meaning the demote-on-alarm safety net the success criteria promise is unavailable.
findings.suggested_fix: In the Context sequencing note, append: 'R7 (stenchful-retail-basenji) must land before any Rn implementation child ships to production, so the trailing FP alarm is active from the first landed recommendation.' Mirror this as a dependency in stenchful-retail-basenji's description.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State explicit ordering constraints or a batched-merge plan between R2/R3/R4 (criteria_routing.json) and between R4/R6 (orchestrator.py) so no child's changes are silently dropped.
evidence: R2 Scope: 'src/rebar/llm/plan_review/det_floor.py + criteria_routing.json (new DET entry, advisory)'
R3 Scope: 'src/rebar/llm/plan_review/criteria_routing.json (new advisory criterion)'
R4 Scope: 'src/rebar/llm/plan_review/ (pass-1 rubric family + the bug-tier claim-gate seam in attest.py/orchestrator) + criteria_routing.json (advisory entry)'
R6 Scope: 'src/rebar/llm/plan_review/orchestrator.py (MOVE_REGISTRY)'
No child states an ordering dependency on any other R-child for these shared files
findings.finding: Children R2 (b080), R3 (3a27), and R4 (03a9) all modify criteria_routing.json with no stated ordering constraints between the three children — creating an implicit shared-state conflict on that file. Similarly, R4 (03a9) and R6 (5dac) both touch the orchestrator (attest.py/orchestrator.py for R4; orchestrator.py MOVE_REGISTRY for R6) with no sequencing stated between them.
scenarios: R2 and R3 land concurrently; both add entries to criteria_routing.json; one overrides the other's change in a merge conflict that is silently resolved by keeping only one file.
R4's orchestrator.py changes conflict with R6's MOVE_REGISTRY additions, causing a merge that drops one change.
findings.suggested_fix: Designate one child (e.g., R4, which is already sequenced after floor-split) as the 'last writer' for criteria_routing.json, or add explicit 'sequenced after R2 and R3' notes to R4's Scope; similarly sequence R6 after R4 for orchestrator.py changes.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document in R1 (59b7) and R5 (ea39) what happens to R1's effectiveness if R5 is deferred, and specify whether R1 should be held or conditionally landed in that scenario.
evidence: R5 What: 'R1's capability probe needs pass-2 verification support: a dedicated asserted_capability_confirmed sub-answer lets the verifier refute capability claims with evidence instead of overloading no_viable_alternative_explanation'
R1 ACs make no mention of R5 as a dependency or of the partial-landing scenario
R5 ACs make no mention of what happens to R1's probe effectiveness if R5 is deferred
findings.finding: R1 (59b7) adds a pass-1 capability grounding probe in the rubric family; R5 (ea39) adds the pass-2 asserted_capability_confirmed sub-answer that provides verification support for exactly that probe. Neither child states a dependency between them: if R5 is deferred (gate fails), R1 lands a probe that fires at pass-1 but receives no pass-2 corroboration, creating a half-implemented detection path where the probe's pass-1 finding can be overridden by an uninformed pass-2 verifier.
scenarios: R5 gate fails (E5 or E6 kappa below threshold) and is deferred; R1 is already landed; the pass-1 probe fires but the pass-2 verifier uses the legacy no_viable_alternative_explanation sub-answer, potentially overriding R1's finding without the dedicated vocabulary — the exact miss scenario R1 is designed to prevent.
A reviewer sees R1's advisory-cap finding overridden at pass-2 for one of the dc58/db7b/5886 historical miss cases because the pass-2 verifier lacks the new sub-answer.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to R1's Scope: 'Note: if R5 is deferred, R1's pass-1 probe operates without pass-2 corroboration; the gate-row writeup for R1 must record this limitation and the team must decide whether to land R1 alone or hold it pending R5.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit back-out plan for `review_kernel` changes: either define a Gerrit-revert procedure with a code-review smoke-test checkpoint, or specify an expand-contract sequence (additive changes only, old path retained until code-review validation completes) so that a post-ship regression has a reversible path.
evidence: 'src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/ (SHARED with code-review - R5 requires the E5 non-regression gate)'
'no config-flag or overlay gating - operator direction 2026-07-15'
ABSENCE: no rollback/back-out plan or expand-contract sequencing described for review_kernel changes
'demote-on-alarm precedent T3/T10' applies only to plan-review FP rate, not to code-review breakage
findings.finding: The plan modifies `src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/`, which is explicitly flagged as SHARED with code-review, but provides no explicit rollback or back-out plan for that shared consumer. The E5 non-regression gate is a pre-ship check, not a post-ship remedy; and the operator-mandated 'no config-flag or overlay gating' policy removes the usual flag-based rollback option. The only post-ship remediation mentioned ('demote-on-alarm' for R7) applies to FP rate on plan-review criteria, not to breakage of the code-review consumer of review_kernel.
findings.location: Context section: 'src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/ (SHARED with code-review - R5 requires the E5 non-regression gate)'; Success Criteria: 'no config-flag or overlay gating - operator direction 2026-07-15'
scenarios: R5 changes to review_kernel pass E5 pre-ship but cause a latent regression in code-review production behavior discovered after merge; with no rollback path defined and no feature flag permitted, the team has no documented procedure to revert or isolate the breakage.
findings.suggested_fix: In the Context or a new 'Rollback' section, add: 'R5 review_kernel changes must be structured as expand-contract: new behaviour is added alongside the existing path; the old path is removed only after a code-review production smoke-test (defined criteria: N consecutive code-review runs with no new FP/FN vs baseline). If the smoke-test fails, the removal commit is reverted via standard Gerrit revert; the addition commit may remain.' This satisfies T4 without violating the no-config-flag directive.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an ADR or design-doc section (e.g., in docs/plan-review-gate.md or a new docs/adr/ entry) capturing the rationale for sharing review_kernel across plan-review and code-review, the interface contract each consumer depends on, and what the E5 non-regression gate is specifically protecting.
evidence: Plan names review_kernel as SHARED with code-review but provides no rationale, interface contract, or isolation description
docs/plan-review-gate.md update is required but scoped to 'every landed change' in plan-review, not to cross-pipeline architecture decisions
No ADR or CLAUDE.md update is mentioned for this coupling
findings.finding: The decision to share `review_kernel` between plan-review and code-review pipelines is a novel cross-component architectural choice, but no ADR or design-doc entry is planned to capture WHY the kernel is shared, what the interface contract is, and what isolation guarantees protect each consumer.
findings.location: Context section: 'src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/ (SHARED with code-review - R5 requires the E5 non-regression gate)'
scenarios: A future engineer modifying review_kernel for code-review needs doesn't know what plan-review contract they must preserve
The E5 gate catches regressions but doesn't document the intended interface — the gate disappears if tests are refactored
findings.suggested_fix: Create docs/adr/review-kernel-shared-pipeline.md (or a §Architecture in docs/plan-review-gate.md) with: (1) decision — kernel is shared to avoid duplication; (2) contract — enumerate the public surface plan-review depends on; (3) isolation guarantee — changes to kernel require E5 gate to pass; (4) consumers — plan_review and code_review with their respective entry points.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that the trailing-FP alarm threshold (currently '>10%') is stored as a named configurable parameter (e.g., in the R7 instrumentation config or docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/ metadata) so it can be adjusted without a source-code change.
evidence: '>10%' appears as a bare numeric constant with no reference to a config key or tunable parameter
R7 dogfood monitoring will likely encode this value somewhere — the plan gives no guidance on where it lives or how to change it
findings.finding: The trailing FP alarm threshold of >10% is stated as a hardcoded policy constant in the plan; if implemented as a literal in monitoring/alerting code it will be difficult to tune post-ship without a code change.
findings.location: Success Criteria: 'alarm at trailing FP >10%, demote-on-alarm precedent T3/T10'
scenarios: The system ships and the FP rate naturally fluctuates around 10% due to corpus shift, requiring threshold adjustment — a code change is needed instead of a config edit
Calibration-3 results suggest a different threshold is appropriate, but the value is buried in monitoring code
findings.suggested_fix: Define a `fp_alarm_threshold` key in the R7 monitoring config (or in criteria_routing.json under a `[monitoring]` section) defaulting to 0.10, and reference that key in the dogfood alarm logic rather than inlining the literal.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Retitle the plan (or add a subtitle/scope note) to reflect that R1-R7 are evaluated and implemented only where gates pass — e.g., 'Evaluate and conditionally implement plan-review decomposition recommendations R1-R7 per SOTA report §6 gates'.
evidence: Title: 'Implement plan-review decomposition recommendations R1-R7'

AC: 'Each recommendation child (R1-R7) is either landed through Gerrit with its gate row demonstrably passed, or closed/deferred with the failing gate result recorded - no recommendation implemented without its gate.'
Success Criteria: 'every rejected/deferred recommendation has the failing result recorded'
findings.finding: The title unconditionally promises 'Implement plan-review decomposition recommendations R1-R7' but the plan body makes implementation conditional on gate-row results, explicitly allowing recommendations to be closed/deferred with failing results recorded. A reader of only the title receives a false expectation that all seven will be implemented.
findings.location: Plan title vs. Success Criteria ('every rejected/deferred recommendation has the failing result recorded') and AC line 3 ('each recommendation child is either landed… or closed/deferred')
scenarios: A stakeholder reading only the title assumes all seven features will ship; the actual deliverable may be a strict subset depending on gate results, leading to misaligned expectations at review time.
findings.suggested_fix: Change title to: 'Evaluate and conditionally implement plan-review decomposition recommendations R1-R7 (task-decomposition SOTA report)' — this matches the body's gate-conditional framing without overpromising full implementation.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Ensure docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md is committed and accessible in the repo (or inline-summarize R1-R7 with their falsifiable gate rows in this plan's body), so that prior-art coverage and any capability assertions within the recommendations can be reviewed before E-gate work begins.
evidence: The file docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md does not exist in the repo snapshot (directory listing for docs/research returned ENOENT).
The ticket englacial-communal-conure/d39b also cannot be resolved (ticket not found error).
The plan body never enumerates what R1-R7 are — all seven recommendations are opaque to any reader of the plan alone.

Success Criteria and all four ACs are gated on 'each recommendation child (R1-R7)' but the plan gives no specification of what these recommendations contain, making prior-art and capability-assertion review impossible at the plan level.
findings.finding: The SOTA report (docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md) that defines R1-R7 and their falsifiable experiments is absent from the repo snapshot; the plan's body never summarizes what R1-R7 actually recommend. Reviewers — and the E-gate implementers — cannot verify whether the report considered relevant prior art (e.g. alternative task-decomposition review techniques), whether any capability assertions within R1-R7 are grounded, or whether any recommendation re-packages an established approach without justification.
findings.location: Plan body — Context section: 'docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md, commit 143d5074e, ticket englacial-communal-conure/d39b' and '§6 defines R1-R7, each with a falsifiable experiment'
scenarios: A reviewer or implementer picks up R3 (for example) and discovers it re-packages an existing technique (e.g. chain-of-thought decomposition critique, already studied in LLM planning literature) with no acknowledgment — no prior-art gap was caught because the SOTA report is inaccessible.

A capability assertion in the SOTA report (e.g. 'technique X achieves Y% FNR improvement') is inherited into an E-gate definition without independent verification, and the E-gate passes on a flawed baseline.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Recommendations summary' section to the plan that enumerates R1-R7 with their gate-row conditions and any capability assertions (e.g. 'R3 asserts that technique X reduces FNR by Y% — sourced from [citation]'). Alternatively, ensure the SOTA report file is committed at the referenced path before this plan is actioned.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
